prerequisites: A Trustpilot Business account with access to the Trustpilot for Business / developer portal.
A registered application on the Trustpilot developer portal, which provides the API Key (Client ID) and Client Secret.
The business unit ID(s) for the Trustpilot business profile(s) you want to extract reviews from (the `business_units` config field).
A `start_date` marking the earliest date from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (access token + refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Trustpilot private APIs require OAuth 2.0. After registering an application you receive an API Key (Client ID) and Client Secret; you exchange these for an access token (valid for 100 hours) and a refresh token (valid for 30 days). The connector stores all four values in its `credentials` field: client_id, client_secret, access_token, and refresh_token. Access tokens are sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, and the refresh token is used to obtain new access tokens when they expire.

setupSteps.title: Register a Trustpilot application
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Trustpilot for Business / developer portal and register an application. Trustpilot issues an API Key, which serves as your Client ID, along with a Client Secret. Record both values; the Client Secret is typically shown only at creation time.

setupSteps.title: Obtain access and refresh tokens
setupSteps.body: Using your Client ID and Client Secret, complete an OAuth 2.0 grant flow to obtain an access token and a refresh token. The access token is valid for 100 hours and the refresh token for 30 days. When the access token expires, request a new one by POSTing the refresh token to https://api.trustpilot.com/v1/oauth/oauth-business-users-for-applications/refresh.

setupSteps.title: Identify your business unit IDs
setupSteps.body: Determine the business unit ID for each Trustpilot business profile you want to pull reviews from, and provide these in the connector's `business_units` field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the `credentials` (client_id, client_secret, access_token, refresh_token), the `business_units` list, and a `start_date` for the earliest data to sync. The connector authenticates with the access token as a Bearer token and refreshes it using the refresh token when it expires.
